classification_rationale: This is a vendored fork of the third-party defi-wonderland/xERC20 (Connext xTokens) cross-chain bridged-token standard, not an AFI protocol artifact. package.json identifies it: name 'xerc20', homepage 'https://github.com/defi-wonderland/xERC20#readme', repository 'git+https://github.com/defi-wonderland/xERC20.git', author 'Wonderland' (package.json:2-12). The audit doc is 'Connext_xTokens_Audit_-_Creed_-_v0.3.pdf' (audits/). Grep for every AFI protocol term (afi, signalId, usignal/USS, cpj/CPJ, reactor, gateway, vault, TSSD, epoch, beneficiary, emissions, mintForSignal, coordinateMint, reputation, registry-as-AFI, replay, determinism, kafka, mongo, postgres, snowflake, doctrine) across all .sol/.md/.json/.ts returns ZERO hits in source. The single AFI-specific change is a one-line OZ v5 Ownable compatibility patch (commit 305922f) whose message states 'Enables AFI Protocol contracts to compile with OZ v5'. The contracts implement generic XERC20 bridge mint/burn rate-limits (XERC20.sol), unrelated to AFI signal->mint commitment semantics. It is infrastructure plumbing the AFI token layer may consume, but it contains no normative AFI surface.
primary_purpose: A vendored fork of the defi-wonderland/xERC20 (Connext xTokens) Solidity standard providing a cross-chain bridged-token implementation (XERC20, XERC20Lockbox, XERC20Factory) with per-bridge rate-limited mint/burn, patched only for OpenZeppelin v5 Ownable compatibility so it can compile alongside AFI Protocol contracts.
protocol_touchpoints: contracts (generic XERC20 bridge token, NOT AFI mint/receipt)
potentially upstream of mint/commitment plane only by reuse of OZ v5 toolchain (no direct AFI mint logic)

upstream: @openzeppelin/contracts (lib/openzeppelin-contracts, branch v4.9.3 per .gitmodules but patched for v5 per commit 305922f)
downstream: AFI commitment/mint layer (afi-token / afi-mint) MAY consume this xERC20 standard for a cross-chain bridgeable AFI token; relationship is implied only by the OZ-v5 patch commit message, no code import exists in this repo

notable_findings.title: Repo is an unmodified third-party Connext/defi-wonderland xERC20 standard, not an AFI artifact
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat as OUT_OF_SCOPE for the portable-protocol surface; document in the repo map that afi-xerc20 is a vendored external standard (bridge token), not AFI normative/reference code, to prevent it being mistaken for the AFI mint/commitment plane.

notable_findings.title: Zero AFI protocol vocabulary present anywhere in source
notable_findings.evidence: grep -rni for afi|signalId|usignal|USS|cpj|reactor|gateway|vault|TSSD|epoch|beneficiary|emissions|mintForSignal|coordinateMint|MintCoordinated|AFIMintCoordinator|AFISignalReceipt|EmissionsMinted|replay|determinism|kafka|mongo|postgres|snowflake|datalake|doctrine across *.sol/*.md/*.json/*.ts returned no hits (only generic 'canonical token' for bridging in README.md:9 and XERC20Deploy.sol:25).
notable_findings.recommendation: Confirms classification: this holds no USS/CPJ ingest, TSSD/vault, AFI mint-receipt, DAG, replay, or registry surface. No normative AFI artifacts to extract.

notable_findings.title: Only AFI-specific change is a 1-line OZ v5 Ownable compatibility patch
notable_findings.evidence: git commit 305922f: 'chore: patch XERC20 for OpenZeppelin Ownable v5 compatibility ... Enables AFI Protocol contracts to compile with OZ v5'; XERC20.sol:37 constructor adds Ownable(msg.sender) then _transferOwnership(_factory).
notable_findings.recommendation: This is the sole AFI integration intent. If the AFI mint/commitment token is meant to be cross-chain via xERC20, document that linkage explicitly in afi-token/afi-mint; otherwise this fork may be a stray/exploratory dependency.

notable_findings.title: .gitmodules pins OZ v4.9.3 but contract was patched for OZ v5 -- toolchain inconsistency
notable_findings.evidence: .gitmodules: [submodule "lib/openzeppelin-contracts"] url ... branch = v4.9.3; contradicts commit 305922f which patches for 'OZ v5 which mandates initialOwner parameter' (XERC20.sol:37).
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag stale: reconcile the OZ submodule pin (v4.9.3) with the v5 compatibility patch, or the v5 patch will not match the vendored v4.9.3 lib at build time. Low impact (no AFI normative surface).

notable_findings.title: BASE (chain 8453) and 12 other chains contain broadcast artifacts for upstream XERC20Factory, not AFI mint contracts
notable_findings.recommendation: Do not mistake these on-chain breadcrumbs for AFI commitment-plane (mint events/receipts/emissions). They are generic bridge-factory deployments inherited from the upstream fork; not part of the AFI signalId/epoch/content-hash anchoring model.
